hero.title: About The Presbyterian Church of Nigeria
hero.subtitle: Transforming Lives, Changing Destinies

origin.title: Rooted in the Reformation
paragraphs: The Presbyterian Church of Nigeria is aware of its origins in the Reformation, especially the enlightening ministries of John Calvin in Switzerland and John Knox in Scotland.
The PCN is indebted to the Presbyterian Church in Jamaica for sending five Jamaicans and Rev. Hope Masterdon Waddell to Calabar in 1846 at the invitation of King Evamba V and King Eyo II.
The PCN First Abuja Parish was inaugurated on 8th April, 1984. It comprises Wuse, Zauda and Jeida Congregations with Mission Stations at Apo, Pegi, Abaji, Ogaminana, Kabusa, Kwali, Piyanko and Wuye.

timeline.event: Rev. Hope Masterdon Waddell and five Jamaicans arrive in Calabar at the invitation of King Evamba V and King Eyo II.
The Presbytery of Biafra is created on September 1st, marking a landmark in Nigerian church history.
Rev. Esien Esien Ukpabio becomes the first indigenous Nigerian minister, ordained on April 9th.
The Synod of Biafra is formed on May 4th, a major step toward church independence.
The church is renamed The Presbyterian Church of Eastern Nigeria in June.
In June, the church adopts its current name - The Presbyterian Church of Nigeria.
PCN First Abuja Parish is inaugurated on 8th April, comprising Wuse, Zauda and Jeida Congregations.

about.vision: To be a Bible-based Church, proclaiming to the world by preaching and example, the good news of the love of God through Jesus Christ His Son under the guidance of the Holy Spirit.
about.mission: To carry the gospel to all parts of Nigeria and beyond through evangelism, discipleship, service and promotion of social righteousness.

doctrines.title: The Holy Scripture
doctrines.description: We believe the Bible is the inspired, infallible Word of God and the supreme authority for faith and practice.

doctrines.title: The Holy Trinity
doctrines.description: We believe in one God, eternally existing in three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.

doctrines.title: Jesus Christ
doctrines.description: We believe in the deity and humanity of Jesus Christ, His virgin birth, sinless life, atoning death, bodily resurrection, and ascension.

doctrines.title: Salvation by Grace
doctrines.description: We believe that salvation is by grace alone, through faith alone, in Christ alone - not by works.

doctrines.title: The Holy Spirit
doctrines.description: We believe in the person and work of the Holy Spirit, who convicts, regenerates, indwells, and empowers believers.

doctrines.title: The Church
doctrines.description: We believe in the universal Church, the body of Christ, composed of all true believers from every nation and generation.

doctrines.title: The Second Coming
doctrines.description: We believe in the personal, visible, and bodily return of Jesus Christ to judge the living and the dead.

doctrines.title: Eternal Life
doctrines.description: We believe in the resurrection of the dead - the saved unto eternal life, and the lost unto eternal separation from God.

mainMinistries.name: Worship Ministry
mainMinistries.tagline: Lifting His Name in Every Service
mainMinistries.description: Leading the congregation in Spirit-filled worship across all services - from the earliest morning gathering to the Teen & Children's Church.

mainMinistries.name: Evangelism, Discipleship & Follow Up Ministry
mainMinistries.description: Active gospel outreach, systematic discipleship and diaspora engagement - taking the Word beyond the walls of the sanctuary.

mainMinistries.name: Prayer & Teaching Ministry
mainMinistries.tagline: The Intercession Engine of the Parish
mainMinistries.description: Fuelling every department through corporate and individual prayer, and grounding the congregation in deep, systematic Bible teaching.

mainMinistries.name: Music Ministry
mainMinistries.tagline: Excellence in Every Note of Praise
mainMinistries.description: Five distinct choral and orchestral ensembles - from the Awesome Choir to the Church Orchestra - lifting worship to the highest standard.

mainMinistries.name: Ushering, Protocol & Follow Up Ministry
mainMinistries.description: Dedicated to orderliness, warm hospitality, and diligent follow-up - ensuring every member and visitor feels the excellence of God's presence.

mainMinistries.name: Media, ICT, Library & Documentation Ministry
mainMinistries.tagline: Bridging Church and the Digital World
mainMinistries.description: End-to-end digital ministry: live streaming, social media, publications, documentation, and managing the parish library and bookshop resources.

mainMinistries.name: Love & Care Ministry
mainMinistries.description: Upholding the spiritual and physical welfare of Parish families - overseeing church arms, organisations, and supporting members through every season of life.

mainMinistries.name: Christian Home & Family Life Ministry
mainMinistries.description: Walking with families through every stage of life - from pre-marriage preparation to post-marriage counselling and sustained family life balance.

mainMinistries.name: Neighbourhood Intervention & Corporate Social Responsibility Ministry
mainMinistries.description: Executing the church's social responsibility through scholarships, community programmes, and strategic interventions in the Wuse host community.

mainMinistries.name: Environment, Aesthetics & Sanctuary Keepers Ministry
mainMinistries.description: Maintaining the beauty, order and sanctity of the church environment - from landscaping and aesthetics to the dedicated sanctuary keepers team.

churchArms.name: Men's Christian Association
churchArms.description: Monthly breakfast meetings hosted by different MCA families, building brotherhood and accountability among men of the parish.

churchArms.name: Women's Guild
churchArms.description: Monthly fellowship every second Saturday and business meetings on the last Thursday - empowering women in faith and purposeful living.

churchArms.name: PYPAN
churchArms.description: The vibrant youth arm of the parish. Fellowships twice monthly on the first and last Friday evenings - raising the next generation.

churchArms.name: Christian Girls in Training
churchArms.description: Raising godly girls through intentional grooming, moral formation, and practical skills development for purposeful living in Christ.

churchArms.name: Boy's Brigade
churchArms.description: Promoting obedience, reverence, discipline and self-respect in young boys through structured, faith-based activities.

outreachArms.name: Children's Department
outreachArms.description: Five age-grouped classes serving 50-100 children regularly with dedicated Sunday school teachers nurturing faith from the earliest years.

outreachArms.name: Teenage Department
outreachArms.description: Investing in teenagers (ages 13-21) for a fruitful future generation through mentorship, discipleship, and fellowship.

outreachArms.name: Friends of the Lost
outreachArms.description: A dedicated support structure for missionaries in the field - connecting the parish to gospel frontlines across Nigeria and beyond.

outreachArms.name: Prison & Hospital Ministry
outreachArms.description: Active outreach to hospitals, prisons, and vulnerable communities - taking the love of Christ to those the world often forgets.
